message:
feat(runtime): conformal alerting and rendering improvements

conformal_alert.rs:
- Add VOI-gated threshold checking for conformal alerts
- Integrate e-process validation for statistical guarantees

subscription.rs:
- Minor adjustment to subscription timing

presenter.rs:
- Improve presentation layer handling

repro_orphan_continuation.rs:
- Update test for orphan continuation scenarios

forms_input.rs:
- Demo form input adjustments

.beads/issues.jsonl:
- Sync issue tracker state

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3cuk","title":"Text Effects: Comprehensive Test Suite","description":"## Background
Text effects are highly visual and easy to break. This bead is the **canonical, aggregated QA gate** for the entire text‑effects system. It does NOT replace per‑effect tests in each feature bead; it **collects them into one coherent test plan** so nothing is forgotten and CI has a single high‑signal target.

## Scope
- Unit tests (core math + every effect type)
- Snapshot tests (visual regression)
- Benchmarks (performance budgets)
- E2E script (headless demo + timing logs)

---

## 1) Unit Tests (crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs #[cfg(test)])

```rust
mod tests {
    // === Easing (10 tests) ===
    test_easing_linear_identity
    test_easing_input_clamped
    test_easing_bounds_normal
    test_easing_elastic_overshoots
    test_easing_back_overshoots
    test_easing_monotonic
    test_easing_step_discrete
    test_easing_in_derivative_zero
    test_easing_out_derivative_zero
    test_easing_symmetry

    // === Color Utilities (6 tests) ===
    test_lerp_color_at_0
    test_lerp_color_at_1
    test_lerp_color_midpoint
    test_apply_alpha_zero
    test_apply_alpha_one
    test_hsv_to_rgb_hue_cycle

    // === Gradients (8 tests) ===
    test_gradient_sample_at_stops
    test_gradient_sample_between
    test_gradient_empty
    test_gradient_single_stop
    test_vertical_gradient_direction
    test_diagonal_gradient_angle
    test_radial_gradient_center
    test_animated_gradient_moves

    // === Core Effects (expanded) ===
    test_fade_in_at_0
    test_fade_in_at_1
    test_fade_out_inverse
    test_pulse_oscillates
    test_organic_pulse_asymmetric
    test_wave_sinusoidal
    test_wave_amplitude
    test_shake_bounded
    test_cascade_order
    test_scramble_resolves
    test_glitch_intensity
    test_reveal_ltr
    test_reveal_mask_softness
    test_cursor_blinks
    test_outline_bounds
    test_mirror_reflection
    test_particle_dissolve_progress
    test_chromatic_split
    test_scanline_pattern
    test_glow_layers
    test_shadow_offset

    // === Effect Chains (8 tests) ===
    test_chain_single_effect
    test_chain_color_blend
    test_chain_alpha_multiply
    test_chain_position_add
    test_chain_char_priority
    test_chain_order_deterministic
    test_chain_clear
    test_chain_max_limit

    // === Presets (6 tests) ===
    test_preset_applies
    test_preset_customizable
    test_preset_all_render
    test_preset_distinct_effects
    test_preset_no_conflicts
    test_preset_effects_method

    // === Sequencer (10 tests) ===
    test_sequence_single_step
    test_sequence_multi_step
    test_sequence_loop
    test_sequence_pingpong
    test_sequence_loop_count
    test_sequence_events
    test_sequence_pause_resume
    test_sequence_reset
    test_sequence_empty
    test_sequence_step_easing

    // === TransitionState (5 tests) ===
    test_transition_start
    test_transition_progress
    test_transition_complete
    test_transition_opacity_curve
    test_transition_with_gradient
}
```

---

## 2) Snapshot Tests (crates/ftui-extras/tests/text_effects_snapshots.rs)

Using ftui-harness to render to virtual buffer and compare:
```rust
#[test] fn snapshot_rainbow_gradient() { ... }
#[test] fn snapshot_wave_frame_0() { ... }
#[test] fn snapshot_wave_frame_10() { ... }
#[test] fn snapshot_glow_3_layer() { ... }
#[test] fn snapshot_ascii_art_block() { ... }
#[test] fn snapshot_outline_solid() { ... }
#[test] fn snapshot_mirror_reflection() { ... }
#[test] fn snapshot_particle_dissolve_mid() { ... }
#[test] fn snapshot_scanline_crt() { ... }
#[test] fn snapshot_preset_neon() { ... }
#[test] fn snapshot_preset_cyberpunk() { ... }
#[test] fn snapshot_effect_chain() { ... }
```

---

## 3) Benchmarks (crates/ftui-extras/benches/text_effects_bench.rs)

```rust
// Performance budgets - PANIC if exceeded
bench_ease_apply          // Budget: 100ns, Panic: 500ns
bench_gradient_sample     // Budget: 50ns,  Panic: 200ns
bench_styled_text_render  // Budget: 1μs/char, Panic: 5μs/char
bench_shadow_render       // Budget: 500ns/char, Panic: 2μs/char
bench_wave_offset         // Budget: 50ns/char, Panic: 200ns/char
bench_effect_chain_8      // Budget: 800ns, Panic: 4μs
```

---

## 4) E2E Script (scripts/demo_text_effects_e2e.sh)

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

echo '=== Text Effects E2E Tests ==='
echo "Date: $(date)"

# Build release
echo 'Building...'
cargo build -p ftui-demo-showcase --release 2>&1 | tail -1

# Run demo in headless mode with timeout
# The demo writes render stats to stderr
echo 'Running visual effects screen...'
timeout 10s cargo run -p ftui-demo-showcase --release -- \
    --screen visual_effects \
    --headless \
    --ticks 100 \
    2>&1 | tee /tmp/text_effects_e2e.log

# Check for panics
if grep -q 'panic' /tmp/text_effects_e2e.log; then
    echo 'FAIL: Panic detected!'
    exit 1
fi

# Check render times
avg_ms=$(grep 'avg_render_ms' /tmp/text_effects_e2e.log | tail -1 | cut -d= -f2)
if (( $(echo "$avg_ms > 16" | bc -l) )); then
    echo "WARN: Render time ${avg_ms}ms exceeds 16ms budget"
fi

echo 'All E2E tests passed!'
```

---

## Files
- crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs (unit tests)
- crates/ftui-extras/tests/text_effects_snapshots.rs (new)
- crates/ftui-extras/benches/text_effects_bench.rs (new)
- scripts/demo_text_effects_e2e.sh (new)

---

## Acceptance
- [ ] All unit tests pass (expanded coverage)
- [ ] Snapshots cover gradients, typography, special FX, presets
- [ ] Benchmarks within budgets
- [ ] E2E script exits 0
- [ ] cargo clippy --all-targets clean
- [ ] cargo fmt --check passes

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Comprehensive Test Suite
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

[PASS] rainbow: 10 frames, 1.2ms avg, 0 errors
...
All 16 effects passed!

 {"id":"bd-3cwi","title":"Performance Benchmark Validation Suite","description":"## Background

FrankenTUI has strict performance requirements as documented in AGENTS.md:
- 16-byte Cell for SIMD comparisons
- Row-major buffer layout for cache prefetching
- Sub-millisecond execution for typical operations
- 64KB buffered output (one write per frame)

This bead creates a comprehensive benchmark suite to validate these requirements.

## Benchmarks to Create

### Cell Operations (crates/ftui-render/benches/cell_bench.rs)
```rust
#[bench] fn cell_comparison_simd_aligned() { /* 4 cells per cache line */ }
#[bench] fn cell_default_construction() { /* <10ns */ }
#[bench] fn cell_clone_16_bytes() { /* verify no heap allocation */ }
```

### Buffer Operations (crates/ftui-render/benches/buffer_bench.rs)
```rust
#[bench] fn buffer_create_80x24() { /* <100μs */ }
#[bench] fn buffer_create_200x50() { /* <500μs */ }
#[bench] fn buffer_fill_80x24() { /* <1ms */ }
#[bench] fn buffer_row_major_scan() { /* verify cache efficiency */ }
```

### Diff Computation (crates/ftui-render/benches/diff_bench.rs)
```rust
#[bench] fn diff_identical_buffers() { /* O(1) fast path */ }
#[bench] fn diff_single_cell_change() { /* <10μs */ }
#[bench] fn diff_full_screen_change_80x24() { /* <1ms */ }
#[bench] fn diff_full_screen_change_200x50() { /* <5ms */ }
```

### Presenter (crates/ftui-render/benches/presenter_bench.rs)
```rust
#[bench] fn presenter_emit_no_change() { /* <1μs */ }
#[bench] fn presenter_emit_single_cell() { /* <10μs */ }
#[bench] fn presenter_emit_full_screen() { /* <5ms */ }
#[bench] fn presenter_state_tracking() { /* verify no redundant SGR */ }
```

### Widget Rendering (crates/ftui-widgets/benches/widget_bench.rs)
```rust
#[bench] fn paragraph_render_100_chars() { /* <100μs */ }
#[bench] fn list_render_100_items() { /* <1ms */ }
#[bench] fn table_render_10x10() { /* <500μs */ }
```

## Performance Budget Enforcement

Create `scripts/bench_budget.sh`:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

BUDGETS=(
    'cell_comparison:100ns'
    'buffer_create_80x24:100μs'
    'diff_full_screen:1ms'
    'presenter_emit_full:5ms'
)

cargo bench --bench '*' -- --save-baseline current 2>/dev/null

for budget in "${BUDGETS[@]}"; do
    name=${budget%%:*}
    limit=${budget##*:}
    actual=$(cargo bench --bench '*' -- $name --noplot 2>/dev/null | grep 'time:' | awk '{print $3}')
    echo "[$name] Budget: $limit, Actual: $actual"
done
```

## CI Integration

Add to .github/workflows/ci.yml:
```yaml
benchmark:
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
    - run: cargo bench --bench '*' -- --noplot
    - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: benchmark-results
        path: target/criterion
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Cell benchmarks created and passing
- [ ] Buffer benchmarks created and passing
- [ ] Diff benchmarks created and passing
- [ ] Presenter benchmarks created and passing
- [ ] Widget benchmarks created and passing
- [ ] bench_budget.sh script working
- [ ] CI job configured (warn-only, not blocking)
- [ ] All benchmarks have documented budgets
- [ ] No benchmark exceeds 2x budget (panic threshold)

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.
- Drift/Regimes: if runtime metrics vary (resize storms, frame pacing), incorporate change-point detection and regime-specific policies.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.
- Add JSONL perf logs with schema: run_id, case, env, features, seed, metrics, budgets, pass/fail, and artifact paths.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.


## E2E Plan
- Provide/extend an E2E script that exercises the feature end-to-end in PTY (where applicable).
- Logging: verbose JSONL with env/capabilities + per-step timings + seed + checksums; include a summary at end.
- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.

 {"id":"bd-3dca","title":"Session Recording to Asciicast Format","description":"# Session Recording to Asciicast Format

## Background & Motivation

Recording terminal sessions enables:
- Bug reports with full reproduction
- Demo videos for documentation
- Shareable examples via asciinema.org

**The solution:**
- Record all terminal output in asciicast v2 format
- Compatible with asciinema-player for web embedding
- Integrate with Time-Travel Debugging for offline replay

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Extends bd-2lwf (Time-Travel Debugging) with export capability
- Enhances bd-2fx (export adapters) with asciicast output

## Technical Design

```rust
pub struct AsciicastRecorder {
    output: BufWriter<File>,
    start_time: Instant,
    terminal_size: (u16, u16),
}

impl AsciicastRecorder {
    pub fn new(path: &Path, width: u16, height: u16) -> io::Result<Self> {
        let header = json!({
            "version": 2,
            "width": width,
            "height": height,
            "timestamp": Utc::now().timestamp()
        });
        // Write header...
    }
    
    pub fn record_output(&mut self, data: &[u8]) {
        let time = self.start_time.elapsed().as_secs_f64();
        let event = json!([time, "o", String::from_utf8_lossy(data)]);
        writeln!(self.output, "{}", event).ok();
    }
    
    pub fn finish(self) -> io::Result<()> {
        self.output.flush()
    }
}
```

## Logging Requirements

```rust
// Recording start (INFO level)
tracing::info!(path = ?path, width = width, height = height, "Asciicast recording started");

// Output recorded (TRACE level)
tracing::trace!(bytes = data.len(), elapsed_secs = time, "Output recorded");

// Recording complete (INFO level)
tracing::info!(path = ?path, duration_secs = duration, events = event_count, "Asciicast recording complete");
```

## E2E Tests

```bash
# Record a session
./ftui-harness --record-asciicast session.cast --duration 10s

# Validate asciicast format
asciinema cat session.cast > /dev/null && echo "Valid format"

# Verify playback
asciinema play session.cast --speed 2

# Test with asciinema-player web component
./scripts/test-asciicast-player.sh session.cast
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Asciicast v2 format output (NDJSON)
- [ ] Plays correctly in asciinema-player
- [ ] Timing preserved accurately (sub-ms precision)
- [ ] Works with large recordings (1hr+)
- [ ] Integration with ftui runtime
- [ ] Input events optionally recorded
- [ ] All tests pass","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:11:11.935650561Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:31:59.489446043Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:31:59.489378395Z","close_reason":"AsciicastRecorder implemented with v2 format, NDJSON output, input/output recording, JSON escaping, and AsciicastLoader for playback (20 tests passing)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3dca","depends_on_id":"bd-2fx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3dca","depends_on_id":"bd-2lwf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3e1t","title":"Alien Artifact Efficiency & Power Upgrade","description":"Background
- FrankenTUI already includes principled control in render budgeting (PID + e-process) and VOI sampling. This epic layers additional mathematically rigorous mechanisms to improve performance and power, especially on large, high-resolution terminals, while preserving determinism and correctness.

Goals
- Reduce diff/present overhead on large screens without sacrificing correctness.
- Improve resize handling under bursty input with formal regime detection.
- Add distribution-free frame-time risk control to prevent budget overruns.
- Keep behavior deterministic and explainable with evidence ledgers.

Non-Goals
- No ML black boxes. No non-deterministic heuristics. No compatibility shims.

Success Criteria
- Rendering remains flicker-free and deterministic in inline and alt-screen modes.
- Performance scales gracefully with large terminal sizes (e.g., 200x60+).
- Unit + property tests validate invariants, and e2e harness scenarios pass.
- All new decisions have explainable, logged evidence (JSONL).

Notes
- API changes are allowed. Prefer explicit configuration toggles with safe defaults.
- Maintain one-writer rule and dirty-row invariants.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:15:57.781614542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:15:57.781614542Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-3e1t.1","title":"Bayesian Diff Strategy + Dirty-Row Fast Path","description":"Background
- Diff computation is currently full row-major scan via BufferDiff::compute.
- Buffer has dirty-row tracking and compute_dirty exists, but runtime path does not exploit it by default.
- On large screens, most frames update a small subset of rows; dirty-row + probabilistic strategy selection can reduce CPU without losing correctness.

Goal
- Choose the cheapest correct diff strategy per frame using expected-loss minimization on an online change-rate posterior.
- Ensure dirty-row invariants are used safely and cleared appropriately.

Key Concepts
- Change-rate posterior p ~ Beta(α, β) on observed per-frame dirty-row fraction or changed-cell fraction.
- Expected cost model compares: full diff vs dirty diff vs full redraw.
- Always deterministic: decisions depend only on past data and current buffer metadata.

Success Criteria
- Output equivalence: diff strategy never changes rendered output.
- Measurable perf wins on large terminals with sparse changes.
- Evidence ledger emitted for each strategy decision.

Notes
- Inline and alt-screen must both benefit (diff path shared in TerminalWriter).
- Keep memory overhead minimal; no per-cell history beyond existing buffers.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:08.753874676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:08.753874676Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:08.753874676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3e1t.1.1","title":"Define diff cost model + Bayesian decision policy","description":"Background
- We can choose between diff strategies per frame: full diff (`BufferDiff::compute`), dirty-row diff (`compute_dirty`), or full redraw.
- Choice should be based on expected cost, not ad-hoc thresholds.

Task
- Specify a cost model for diff+emit: cost = c_scan * N_cells_scanned + c_emit * N_cells_changed + c_overhead.
- Define how to estimate change-rate posterior `p` from observed dirty rows or changed cells.
- Define expected cost for each strategy and the decision rule: pick argmin expected cost.

Details
- Posterior: p ~ Beta(α, β). Update each frame using observed change fraction (dirty rows or changed cells).
- Use `E[p]` and optional upper-quantile (conservative) for worst-case inlining.
- Decide when to fall back to full redraw (e.g., p above threshold or buffer dimension mismatch).

Acceptance Criteria
- Written spec in issue description is sufficient to implement without extra docs.
- Decision rule is deterministic and uses only past data and current buffer metadata.

Notes
- Keep the model simple (O(1) update) to avoid adding overhead.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:35.634169961Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:35.634169961Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:35.634169961Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3e1t.1.2","title":"Implement dirty-row diff path + dirty state lifecycle","description":"Background
- `Buffer` tracks dirty rows and `BufferDiff::compute_dirty` exists.
- Runtime currently uses full diff in `TerminalWriter::present_*`.

Task
- Integrate `compute_dirty` into the presentation path while preserving output correctness.
- Establish dirty-row lifecycle: when to clear dirty flags so subsequent frames are correct.

Implementation Notes
- Use dirty rows from the new frame (the buffer produced by view/render).
- After a diff is computed and emitted, clear dirty flags on the new buffer before storing as prev.
- Handle dimension mismatch by forcing full diff and marking all dirty.

Acceptance Criteria
- Output equivalence proven via unit tests (dirty diff vs full diff).
- No regressions in inline or alt-screen.

Risks
- Forgetting to clear dirty rows could cause under-diffing later. Tests must guard.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:47.490619370Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:47.490619370Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:47.490619370Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3e1t.1.3","title":"Implement change-rate posterior (Beta) + expected-cost evaluation","description":"Background
- We need a lightweight online estimator to decide among diff strategies.

Task
- Implement Beta posterior updates for change rate `p`.
- Provide expected-cost functions for each diff strategy using the cost model from A1.
- Store minimal state inside `TerminalWriter` (or adjacent module) with deterministic updates.

Considerations
- Use dirty-row fraction as a proxy for change fraction when full diff is avoided.
- Optionally update posterior using actual changed-cell fraction when full diff is computed.
- Clamp p to avoid degenerate 0/1 extremes; keep α,β >= ε.

Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic updates, no allocations in hot path.
- Decision can be logged with contributing terms (p estimate, costs).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:53.638516835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:53.638516835Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:53.638516835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3e1t.1.4","title":"Wire strategy selection into TerminalWriter (inline + alt)","description":"Background
- After implementing dirty-row diff and change-rate posterior, we must integrate a unified decision point in presentation.

Task
- Add a strategy selector in `TerminalWriter` that chooses: full diff, dirty diff, or full redraw, based on expected cost and safe constraints.
- Ensure selection is identical for inline and alt-screen paths.

Constraints
- Must remain deterministic; no randomness.
- Must handle dimension changes by forcing full diff/redraw.
- Keep changes localized to `TerminalWriter` to avoid API churn unless needed.

Acceptance Criteria
- One decision path used for both modes.
- Logs include chosen strategy + cost breakdown (for evidence ledger).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:00.301691177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:00.301691177Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:17:00.301691177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3e1t.2","title":"BOCPD Resize Coalescer Upgrade","description":"Background
- Resize coalescer currently uses a heuristic burst/steady regime with hysteresis thresholds.
- We want a mathematically principled, adaptive regime detector with formal properties.

Goal
- Replace heuristic regime detection with Bayesian Online Change-Point Detection (BOCPD) to classify steady vs burst behavior.
- Preserve existing invariants: latest-wins, bounded latency, deterministic decisions.

Key Concepts
- Run-length posterior over regime changes, hazard function for change probability.
- Decision uses posterior mass over burst regime to choose coalescing delay.
- Computation kept O(K) with run-length truncation.

Success Criteria
- Coalescer remains deterministic and respects hard deadlines.
- Fewer wasted renders during resize storms with no additional latency in steady mode.
- Decision logs include posterior summary for auditability.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:14.516666595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:14.516666595Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:14.516666595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3e1t.3","title":"Conformal Frame-Time Predictor for Budget","description":"Background
- Budget controller currently uses PID + e-process to decide degrade/upgrade.
- We want distribution-free risk control for frame-time overruns, especially on large screens.

Goal
- Add a conformal predictor that provides (1-α) coverage for frame-time risk and gates degradation decisions.
- Keep behavior deterministic, light-weight, and compatible with existing budget controller.

Key Concepts
- Nonconformity score based on frame-time residuals.
- Sliding window or exponential decay to adapt to drift.
- Quantile-based threshold to ensure coverage without distribution assumptions.

Success Criteria
- Empirically validate coverage in simulation tests.
- No regressions in render cadence; degrade decisions become more reliable under heavy load.
- Configurable α with safe defaults.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:20.724471596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:20.724471596Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:20.724471596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3e1t.4","title":"Evidence Ledgers, Diagnostics, and Large-Screen Benchmarks","description":"Background
- The new math-heavy policies must be explainable and testable.
- Large-screen performance needs dedicated coverage.

Goal
- Provide galaxy-brain-style evidence ledgers for diff, resize, and budget decisions (JSONL, deterministic).
- Add benchmark/scenario coverage for large terminals and bursty inputs.

Success Criteria
- Each policy emits structured, self-explanatory decision records.
- Bench/scenario tests capture large-screen behavior and fail on regressions.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:25.924766023Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:25.924766023Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:16:25.924766023Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e8","title":"Implement structured logging with tracing","description":"INFRASTRUCTURE - LOGGING & TRACING

## Background
Comprehensive logging is critical for debugging terminal rendering issues, performance analysis, and understanding execution flow. Must support both development debugging and production diagnostics.

## Technical Requirements

### Tracing Infrastructure
- Use tracing crate for structured, async-aware logging
- Log levels: ERROR, WARN, INFO, DEBUG, TRACE
- Span-based instrumentation for render pipeline
- Subscriber configuration for:
  - Development: pretty-printed to stderr
  - Testing: captured for assertions
  - Production: JSON structured output (optional)

### Key Instrumentation Points
- Frame lifecycle: start → render → diff → present → complete
- Widget rendering: widget type, area, render time
- Buffer operations: resize, clear, set (at TRACE level)
- Input parsing: raw bytes → parsed events
- State changes: model updates, command execution
- Error conditions: with full context

### Log Format
Development:
  2026-01-31T12:00:00.123Z INFO ftui::render frame_start frame=42 widgets=15
  2026-01-31T12:00:00.125Z DEBUG ftui::diff changed_cells=120 skip_rows=10
  
Testing (structured capture):
  LogEntry { level: INFO, target: "ftui::render", msg: "frame_start", ... }

### Feature Gates
- feature = "tracing" - enables all instrumentation
- feature = "tracing-json" - JSON subscriber for production
- Default: tracing disabled for minimal overhead

## Design Rationale
Terminal UIs are notoriously hard to debug. Structured tracing with spans enables understanding complex render pipelines. Feature-gated to avoid overhead in release builds.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tracing subscriber configuration
- [ ] Frame-level span instrumentation
- [ ] Widget render spans with timing
- [ ] Input parsing tracing
- [ ] Error context capture
- [ ] Test log capture mechanism
- [ ] Feature gate documentation
- [ ] Example showing log output

## Performance Budget
- Disabled: zero overhead (compiled out)
- Enabled: < 1µs per span enter/exit

## Location: ftui-core crate (feature = "tracing")","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:27:56.000241382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T17:54:34.427170543Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T17:54:34.427143291Z","close_reason":"Implemented structured logging with tracing crate. Created logging.rs with init_dev(), init_json(), no-op macros when disabled. Added tracing/tracing-json feature flags. All 71 tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e8","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3emq","title":"Async Validation: Deterministic Concurrency Contract + Golden Trace","description":"## Goal
Make async validation behavior deterministic under a seeded scheduler and prove no stale results can override newer input.

## Core Idea (Extreme Optimization + Correctness)
- Monotonic validation token per input change; results must include token.
- Apply result only if token matches current input token.
- Record validation events in a trace (start/cancel/complete/apply).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Stale results are always ignored with explicit log entries.
- Deterministic trace under fixed seed and scripted input.
- Golden trace checksum matches after refactors (isomorphism proof).

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_token_monotonic: tokens strictly increase per input.
- unit_stale_result_ignored: older token never applies.
- unit_trace_contains_all_events: no missing transitions.

## E2E (REQUIRED)
- Seeded async validator test harness with golden trace hash.
- Log: token, state, duration, decision per validation.

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

 {"id":"bd-3fu8","title":"Semantic Events: Core Types and SemanticEvent Enum","description":"## Task

Define the core `SemanticEvent` enum and related types in `ftui-core`.

## Implementation

Create `crates/ftui-core/src/semantic_event.rs`:

```rust
use crate::{KeyCode, Modifiers, MouseButton, Position};
use std::time::Duration;

/// High-level semantic events derived from raw terminal input.
/// These represent user intentions rather than raw input.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
pub enum SemanticEvent {
    // === Mouse Gestures ===
    
    /// Single click (mouse down + up in same position)
    Click {
        pos: Position,
        button: MouseButton,
    },
    
    /// Two clicks within threshold time
    DoubleClick {
        pos: Position,
        button: MouseButton,
    },
    
    /// Three clicks within threshold (often used for line selection)
    TripleClick {
        pos: Position,
        button: MouseButton,
    },
    
    /// Mouse held down beyond threshold without moving
    LongPress {
        pos: Position,
        duration: Duration,
    },
    
    // === Drag Gestures ===
    
    /// Mouse moved beyond drag threshold while button held
    DragStart {
        pos: Position,
        button: MouseButton,
    },
    
    /// Ongoing drag movement
    DragMove {
        start: Position,
        current: Position,
        delta: (i16, i16),  // Movement since last DragMove
    },
    
    /// Mouse released after drag
    DragEnd {
        start: Position,
        end: Position,
    },
    
    /// Drag cancelled (Escape pressed, focus lost, etc.)
    DragCancel,
    
    // === Keyboard Gestures ===
    
    /// Key chord sequence completed (e.g., Ctrl+K Ctrl+C)
    Chord {
        sequence: Vec<ChordKey>,
    },
    
    // === Touch-Like Gestures ===
    
    /// Swipe gesture (rapid mouse movement)
    Swipe {
        direction: SwipeDirection,
        distance: u16,
        velocity: f32,  // cells per second
    },
}

/// A key in a chord sequence
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub struct ChordKey {
    pub code: KeyCode,
    pub modifiers: Modifiers,
}

/// Direction for swipe gestures
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum SwipeDirection {
    Up,
    Down,
    Left,
    Right,
}

impl SemanticEvent {
    /// Returns true if this is a drag-related event
    pub fn is_drag(&self) -> bool {
        matches\!(self, Self::DragStart { .. } | Self::DragMove { .. } | Self::DragEnd { .. } | Self::DragCancel)
    }
    
    /// Returns the position if this event has one
    pub fn position(&self) -> Option<Position> {
        match self {
            Self::Click { pos, .. } |
            Self::DoubleClick { pos, .. } |
            Self::TripleClick { pos, .. } |
            Self::LongPress { pos, .. } |
            Self::DragStart { pos, .. } => Some(*pos),
            Self::DragMove { current, .. } => Some(*current),
            Self::DragEnd { end, .. } => Some(*end),
            _ => None,
        }
    }
}
```

## Unit Tests

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn semantic_event_is_drag() {
        assert\!(SemanticEvent::DragStart { 
            pos: Position { x: 0, y: 0 }, 
            button: MouseButton::Left 
        }.is_drag());
        
        assert\!(\!SemanticEvent::Click { 
            pos: Position { x: 0, y: 0 }, 
            button: MouseButton::Left 
        }.is_drag());
    }

    #[test]
    fn semantic_event_position() {
        let click = SemanticEvent::Click { 
            pos: Position { x: 5, y: 10 }, 
            button: MouseButton::Left 
        };
        assert_eq\!(click.position(), Some(Position { x: 5, y: 10 }));
        
        let chord = SemanticEvent::Chord { sequence: vec\![] };
        assert_eq\!(chord.position(), None);
    }

    #[test]
    fn chord_key_equality() {
        let k1 = ChordKey { code: KeyCode::Char('k'), modifiers: Modifiers::CONTROL };
        let k2 = ChordKey { code: KeyCode::Char('k'), modifiers: Modifiers::CONTROL };
        let k3 = ChordKey { code: KeyCode::Char('c'), modifiers: Modifiers::CONTROL };
        
        assert_eq\!(k1, k2);
        assert_ne\!(k1, k3);
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] SemanticEvent enum with all variants
- [ ] ChordKey and SwipeDirection types
- [ ] Helper methods (is_drag, position)
- [ ] Unit tests passing
- [ ] Exported from ftui-core lib.rs
- [ ] Documentation with examples

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## E2E Plan
- Provide/extend an E2E script that exercises the feature end-to-end in PTY (where applicable).
- Logging: verbose JSONL with env/capabilities + per-step timings + seed + checksums; include a summary at end.
- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.
